description: An IT Service Manager needs an SLA compliance report where standard SQL timestamp subtraction is insufficient because it includes weekends, holidays, and non-business hours. Using the HR database (localhost:1521/XEPDB1, user: hr, pass: hr123), analyze the SUPPORT_TICKETS and PUBLIC_HOLIDAYS tables to create a view named SLA_PERFORMANCE_VW. This view must calculate the business minutes elapsed between OPENED_AT and CLOSED_AT for each ticket, adhering to business hours of Monday-Friday, 09:00 to 17:00, excluding weekends and dates in the PUBLIC_HOLIDAYS table. Tickets opening after 17:00 should start counting at 09:00 the next business day, and those opening before 09:00 should start at 09:00 that day. The view must include columns for TICKET_ID, PRIORITY, OPENED_AT, CLOSED_AT, TOTAL_HOURS (wall clock), BUSINESS_MINUTES (calculated), and SLA_STATUS (defined as 'MET' if <= 480 mins, otherwise 'BREACHED'). Export the resulting view content to /home/ga/Desktop/sla_report.csv.
